mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stable housing starts indicate robust aggregate demand and household balance sheet health, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over a 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macroeconomic environment, month-over-month growth in the industrial production index signals tightening capacity utilization and expanding corporate pricing power, which compresses valuation multiples and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accelerating above a 2.5% annualized rate confirms underlying economic momentum, which directly supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns average negative values during quarters where GDP growth exceeds 2.5%, or if the rank correlation between GDP growth surprises and SPX returns remains below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where CPI rises while the federal funds rate remains flat creates negative real interest rates, which historically triggers portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward inflation hedges, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% MoM while FEDFUNDS unchanged
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical occurrences, or the cross-product term in a factor model shows a positive coefficient during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect underlying labor market tightness, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day window.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observation periods, or the rolling 12-month Sharpe ratio of the strategy drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle demand slowdown, a simultaneous decline in housing starts and a rise in the unemployment rate signals a breakdown in household spending power, which typically precedes corporate earnings downgrades and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint trigger fails to precede SPX drawdowns of > 3% within 20 days in at least 60% of historical instances, or the signal's hit rate over a 10-year out-of-sample test falls below 40%.
